questions.question: Which ancient Indian university was known for its renowned scholars and attracted students from different parts of Asia during the 5th century CE?
questions.explanation: Nalanda was one of the world's first residential universities and a famous Buddhist learning center during the 5th century CE.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after his victory at the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Which movement was primarily led by Mahatma Gandhi to oppose the Rowlatt Act and promote nonviolent resistance?
questions.explanation: The Non-violent Satyagraha was Gandhi's method of protest against the Rowlatt Act, emphasizing nonviolent resistance.

questions.question: Which document laid the foundation of Indian independence and was signed on August 15, 1947?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act 1947 was an Act of the UK Parliament that partitioned British India into India and Pakistan.

questions.question: Who was the last Governor-General of India before independence?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari was the last Governor-General of India, serving until independence in 1947.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in India, flowing over 1,400 km primarily in central India.

questions.question: Which Indian state is the largest producer of tea?
questions.explanation: Assam is the largest tea-producing state in India, known for its vast tea gardens.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is primarily located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is mainly situated in Rajasthan, covering parts of Gujarat and Punjab as well.

questions.question: Which resource is primarily found in the Saurashtra region of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Saurashtra region is known for its petroleum reserves.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since July 25, 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Surya Kant has been serving as the Chief Justice of India since 24 November 2025.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 14 to 18 in the Indian Constitution provide for the fundamental rights.

questions.question: Who is the current Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Om Birla has been the Speaker of the Lok Sabha since June 19, 2019.

questions.question: Which institution has the power of judicial review in India?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India has the power of judicial review to assess the constitutionality of laws.

questions.question: Which Panchayati Raj institution is responsible for the preparation of development plans at the district level?
questions.explanation: District Panchayat is responsible for district-level planning and development.

questions.question: Who is responsible for presenting the Union Budget in India?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister of India presents the Union Budget annually.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the most to India's GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector is the largest contributor to India's GDP, accounting for over 50%.

questions.question: Which organization is responsible for regulating and supervising banks in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the central bank responsible for regulation and supervision of banks.

questions.question: What was the main objective of the New Economic Policy (1991) reforms?
questions.explanation: The 1991 reforms aimed at liberalizing the economy, privatization, and integrating with the global economy.

questions.question: Which part of the human body is primarily affected by the deficiency of Hemoglobin?
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin deficiency leads to anemia, affecting the blood's capacity to carry oxygen.

questions.question: Which element is most abundant in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust, primarily found as oxides.

questions.question: Which law explains the relationship between pressure and volume of a gas at constant temperature?
questions.explanation: Boyle's Law states that at constant temperature, pressure and volume are inversely proportional.

questions.question: Which space agency launched the Mars Orbiter Mission 'Mangalyaan'?
questions.explanation: ISRO launched the Mars Orbiter Mission 'Mangalyaan' in 2013.

questions.question: Which biodiversity hotspot is located entirely within India?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats is a biodiversity hotspot located entirely within India.

questions.question: Which law aims to protect endangered species in India?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, aims to protect and conserve endangered species.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable, unlike fossil fuels like coal, natural gas, and petroleum.

questions.question: Which phenomenon is primarily responsible for global climate change?
questions.explanation: The Greenhouse Effect, enhanced by human activities, is the main cause of global climate change.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been serving as Prime Minister since 2014.

questions.question: Which Indian scheme aims to improve rural sanitation and eliminate open defecation?
questions.explanation: Swachh Bharat Abhiyan is focused on sanitation and eliminating open defecation.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India since 2019.
